message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

# SP_0002.1 — SDUI Core: Interpolation Parser

  >   resolveContextPath (nested path + array index support)
  >   interpolateString ({{var}} regex replacement)
  >   interpolateDeep (recursive object/array walk)
  > 24 unit tests passing (interpolation.test.ts).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIInterpolation.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Must resolve nested paths: {{product.name}}, {{balance.pointsFormatted}}
- Must deep-walk an object interpolating all string values in props
- Missing values should resolve gracefully (empty string, not crash)
- Existing interpolation example in RFC:
  "Voucher para {{product.name}}" + context.product.name = "Abastecimento"
  → "Voucher para Abastecimento"
- Types from SP_0002.0 should be used

Deliverable: Interpolation utility with unit tests.

- [DONE] Build the rendering engine that turns a JSON screen definition into React
      components. This is the core of the SDUI architecture (RFC sections 3.1–3.2).
  > Registry at src/services/sdui/registry/index.ts — createRegistry, registerComponent,
  >   getRenderer, registerToDefault (immutable API + mutable default singleton).
  > ScreenRenderer at src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx — renders header,
  >   body[], footer from SDUIScreen JSON. Applies interpolateDeep to all props.
  > FallbackComponent at src/services/sdui/renderer/FallbackComponent.tsx — visible
  >   yellow box for unknown component types (uses RN primitives for ThemeProvider safety).
  > 14 unit tests passing (registry.test.ts + ScreenRenderer.test.tsx).
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIScreenRenderer.stories.tsx.
  > Screenshot validated via Playwright.

Context:
- Registry maps string type → React component (RFC section 3.2 "Componentes")
- Renderer receives SDUIScreen JSON, iterates body[], renders each via registry
- Must apply interpolation (SP_0002.1) to all string props using screen context
- Unknown types should render a visible fallback (not crash)
- Types from SP_0002.0, interpolation from SP_0002.1

Deliverable: A <ScreenRenderer screen={json} /> component that renders a
screen definition from JSON.

# SP_0003.2 — Storybook JSON Editor Addon (IndexedDB Persistence)
- [DONE] Create a custom Storybook addon panel that allows editing the SDUI JSON
      payload in real-time, with automatic persistence to IndexedDB.
  > 6 files in .storybook/addons/json-editor/:
  >   register.tsx (addon registration), JsonEditorPanel.tsx (editor UI),
  >   useSDUIJsonEditor.ts (preview-side hook), useIndexedDB.ts (persistence),
  >   constants.ts (shared IDs/events), index.ts (barrel exports).
  > Features: textarea with monospace font, Format/Reset buttons, debounced
  >   auto-save to IndexedDB keyed by story ID, JSON validation with inline errors,
  >   "edited" badge when payload differs from default, live preview via channel events.
  > Integrated in main.ts addons array + used by SDUIDemoVoucher stories.
  > Review fixes applied: IDB error handling (.catch), db.close() on cleanup,
  >   debounce timer cleanup on unmount.

Context:
- Every SDUI story should have a companion JSON editor panel

  a. Show the current SDUIScreen JSON in a textarea or code editor

  e. Have a "Reset to Default" button to restore the original mock payload
- Implementation approach:
  a. Create .storybook/addons/json-editor/ with:

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Component props validation (required fields, known prop names) --- ## Interconnection Map ``` JsonEditorPanel (manager addon) ├─ CodeMirrorEditor (UI) │ ├─ sduiLinter (linting) │ │ └─ sduiSchema (validation rules) │ └─ onValidationChange callback │ ├─ useIndexedDB (persistence) │ └─ IndexedDB API │ └─ Storybook Channel (communication) └─ EVENTS constants └─ JSON transmission: UPDATE_JSON, SET_DEFAULT_JSON, REQUEST_DEFAULT useSDUIJsonEditor (preview hook) ↔ Storybook Channel ↔ JsonEditorPanel ├─ Listens for UPDATE_JSON ├─ Emits SET_DEFAULT_JSON └─ Returns edited SDUIScreen SDUIScreen (type system) ├─ SDUIComponent[] (body, header, footer) ├─ SDUIContext (runtime variables) ├─ SDUIConditional (visibility/enable rules) ├─ SDUIValidation (field validation) └─ SDUIRichText (formatted text) Adapters (renderers) └─ Registry of SDUIComponentRenderer implementations └─ Called by app to render SDUIComponent → React Native UI ``` --- ## Current Validation Architecture **Two-tier validation:** 1. **Syntax layer** (CodeMirrorEditor) - JSON5 parse attempt with detailed error locations - 300ms debounce 2. **Schema layer** (sduiLinter + sduiSchema) - Full JSON Schema validation via AJV - Component-specific prop validation - Smart error messages with suggestions - 500ms debounce **Key Insight:** The schema validation is currently **AJV-based** with custom component prop checking. It validates against `sduiScreenSchema` which enforces: - Required screen fields: `id`, `body` - Known component types - Component-required props (e.g., `heading` requires `text`) - Known props per component type **Ready for enhancement:** The linting architecture is well-structured and can easily accept additional validation rules (e.g., JSON Logic rule validation, interpolation validation, recursive type checking).

4. `.storybook/addons/json-editor/__tests__/sduiLinter.test.ts` — 17 unit tests

Read all 4 files and review for:
- Code quality and correctness
- Security issues
- Performance concerns (AJV compile, Levenshtein on every keystroke)

content.text:
---

## PR Review: SP_1BGXJ78.2 -- JSON Schema validation with inline diagnostics

**Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES**

Overall this is solid work -- the Levenshtein "did you mean" UX is a nice touch, the AJV compilation is correctly done at module level (not per-keystroke), and the test coverage hits the main paths. However, there are several issues ranging from a potential runtime crash to performance concerns and missing edge cases.

---

### CRITICAL / BUGS

**1. `findJsonPath` array index navigation is broken for indices > 0**
`sduiLinter.ts:57-73`

The array traversal logic has a subtle bug. When `currentIdx === -1` and the first non-whitespace character is encountered, it sets `currentIdx = 0` and then immediately checks `if (currentIdx === idx) break` -- but only when `idx === 0`. For `idx >= 1`, the logic relies on counting commas at `bracketDepth === 0`, but the initial `currentIdx` is `-1` and only gets bumped to `0` inside the non-whitespace branch. This means the first comma bumps it from `-1` to `0` (wrong -- should go to `1`), causing off-by-one errors for indices >= 2.

Additionally, the fallback at lines 70-73 (`lastBrace = doc.lastIndexOf('{', pos)`) searches backwards from `pos`, which could match a brace from a completely different branch of the JSON tree if the target index doesn't exist. This would produce a diagnostic pointing at the wrong location.

**Impact:** Diagnostic squiggles will point at wrong locations for body items after the second element. This is a usability bug, not a data-integrity bug, but it undermines the core value proposition of this feature.

**Suggestion:** Consider using a proper JSON source-map library (e.g., `jsonc-parser` from VS Code, which provides `getLocation` / `findNodeAtOffset`). The manual offset tracking is fragile with JSON5 quirks (trailing commas, unquoted keys, comments).

---

**2. `spacingEnum` is declared but never used**
`sduiSchema.ts:6-8`

The `spacingEnum` array is defined but never referenced in any schema property. The `spacingProp` at line 10-13 only declares `type: 'string'` with `nullable: true` -- it does not use `{ enum: spacingEnum }`. This means the schema will not validate that spacing values are one of the allowed tokens.

**Impact:** Invalid spacing values like `mt: "bananas"` will pass validation silently, defeating the purpose of having the enum.

**Fix:** Either use `spacingEnum` in `spacingProp`:
```ts
const spacingProp = {
  type: 'string' as const,
  nullable: true as const,
  enum: spacingEnum,
};
```
Or remove the dead code.

---

**3. `conditionalSchema` has `additionalProperties: false` but no `properties` for non-`rule` fields**
`sduiSchema.ts:15-22`

The conditional schema only allows `rule` and forbids everything else. If the RFC evolves to include `message`, `fallback`, or other fields on conditionals, this will produce confusing AJV errors. Not blocking, but worth a `// TODO` or making it more permissive for forward-compatibility.

---

### PERFORMANCE

**4. Levenshtein runs on every validated keystroke with O(n*m) per candidate**
`sduiLinter.ts:10-22, 24-40`

The `didYouMean` function iterates over all candidates and computes a full Levenshtein matrix for each. With `knownTypes` having ~13 entries and `knownProps` up to ~10 entries per component, this is fine today. But `levenshtein()` allocates a new 2D array on every call (`Array.from` + `.fill`).

**Suggestion:** Use a single-row DP approach to reduce GC pressure:
```ts
function levenshtein(a: string, b: string): number {
  const m = a.length, n = b.length;
  let prev = Array.from({ length: n + 1 }, (_, i) => i);
  for (let i = 1; i <= m; i++) {
    const curr = [i];
    for (let j = 1; j <= n; j++) {
      curr[j] = a[i-1] === b[j-1] ? prev[j-1] : 1 + Math.min(prev[j], curr[j-1], prev[j-1]);
    }
    prev = curr;
  }
  return prev[n];
}
```
Not blocking, but a quick win.

**5. Schema linter delay is 500ms, JSON5 parse linter is 300ms -- they run independently**
`CodeMirrorEditor.tsx:269, 272`

Because these are separate `linter()` instances, there's a window where the schema linter validates stale parsed JSON. For example: user types invalid JSON, the JSON5 linter fires at 300ms and shows a parse error, then the schema linter fires at 500ms on the *previous* valid parse (from AJV's perspective it returns empty since `validateSduiJson` returns `[]` on parse failure). This is actually correct behavior because `validateSduiJson` catches parse errors and returns `[]`. But it means there's a brief flash where schema diagnostics disappear 200ms before parse errors appear. Minor UX issue.

---

### TYPE SAFETY

**6. Heavy use of `any` casts in `humanizeMessage`**
`sduiLinter.ts:89-121`

The function casts `error.data`, `params.missingProperty`, `params.additionalProperty`, `params.type`, and `params.allowedValue` all through `as any`. AJV provides typed error params -- use `Ajv.ErrorObject<string, Record<string, any>>` or the specific keyword param types from `ajv/dist/types` to get proper typing.

**7. `JSONSchemaType` import is unused**
`sduiSchema.ts:1`

The import `import type { JSONSchemaType } from 'ajv'` is brought in but never used. The `sduiScreenSchema` is typed as a plain object literal, not `JSONSchemaType<T>`. Either type the schema properly or remove the import.

---

### EDGE CASES / MISSING COVERAGE

**8. `findJsonPath` does not handle JSON5 unquoted keys correctly**
`sduiLinter.ts:76`

The regex `(?:"${escaped}"|${escaped})\\s*:` will match unquoted keys, but JSON5 also allows single-quoted keys (`'type': 'heading'`). The regex won't match those.

**9. No test for `rich_text` with `children` array and `oneOf` validation**
The `rich_text` component has the most complex schema (with `oneOf` children types). There's no test verifying that invalid children produce diagnostics. This is the most likely schema to have AJV compilation issues with `oneOf`.

**10. No test for `header` / `footer` validation**
Tests only exercise `body` components. The `validateComponentProps` function also validates `header` and `footer` (lines 128-129), but this path is untested.

**11. No test for empty body array**
`{ id: "test", body: [] }` -- is this valid? The schema doesn't set `minItems: 1`, so it passes. But should it? Worth an explicit test to document the decision.

**12. No test for `null` props values**
`sduiLinter.ts:162` checks `props[req] === null`, treating `null` as missing. This is correct for SDUI but deviates from JSON Schema semantics where `null` is a valid value. Worth a test to pin this behavior.

**13. Duplicate diagnostic suppression may hide distinct errors at the same position**
`sduiLinter.ts:241-246`

The dedup key is `${d.from}:${d.message}`. If AJV produces two errors with the same message at the same position but different severities, one will be silently dropped. This could happen with the `allOf` + `if/then` pattern producing both an error and a warning at the same `from`. Consider including severity in the key.

---

### STYLE / MINOR

**14. `knownTypes` is spread unnecessarily**
`sduiSchema.ts:4` -- `const knownTypes = [...SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES]` creates a mutable copy, but `knownTypes` is never mutated. This loses the `as const` typing. Either keep it `readonly` or just use `SDUI_KNOWN_TYPES` directly.

**15. `CodeMirrorEditor.tsx` uses `javascript()` language mode for JSON**
`CodeMirrorEditor.tsx:298`

CodeMirror has `@codemirror/lang-json` which provides better JSON-specific parsing, including proper syntax errors for trailing commas (which JSON5 allows but standard JSON doesn't). Since this editor is explicitly JSON5-aware, using `javascript()` is acceptable, but it means the tree-sitter grammar won't flag structural JSON issues. This is a conscious trade-off -- just worth noting.

---

### SUMMARY

| # | File | Line(s) | Severity | Issue |
|---|------|---------|----------|-------|
| 1 | sduiLinter.ts | 57-73 | bug | Array index navigation off-by-one for index >= 2 |
| 2 | sduiSchema.ts | 6-8 | bug | `spacingEnum` declared but never used in schema |
| 3 | sduiSchema.ts | 15-22 | nit | `conditionalSchema` overly restrictive for future fields |
| 4 | sduiLinter.ts | 24-40 | perf | Levenshtein allocates 2D array per call; use single-row DP |
| 5 | CodeMirrorEditor.tsx | 269,272 | minor | Independent linter timers cause brief diagnostic flicker |
| 6 | sduiLinter.ts | 89-121 | type-safety | Pervasive `as any` casts on AJV error params |
| 7 | sduiSchema.ts | 1 | cleanup | Unused `JSONSchemaType` import |
| 8 | sduiLinter.ts | 76 | edge-case | Regex misses single-quoted JSON5 keys |
| 9 | tests | -- | coverage | No test for `rich_text` `oneOf` children validation |
| 10 | tests | -- | coverage | No test for `header`/`footer` validation path |
| 11 | tests | -- | coverage | No test for empty body array |
| 12 | tests | -- | coverage | No test for `null` prop values treated as missing |
| 13 | sduiLinter.ts | 241-246 | edge-case | Dedup key missing severity, may suppress distinct errors |
| 14 | sduiSchema.ts | 4 | nit | Unnecessary spread loses `as const` typing |

**Blocking items:** #1 (incorrect diagnostic positions) and #2 (dead `spacingEnum` / missing validation). Everything else is non-blocking but should be addressed before or shortly after merge.

================================================================================

but no runtime code. They complete the SDUI engine to full RFC spec.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [ ] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.1 — Component Visibility & Button Enabled Conditionals
- [ ] Wire the JSON Logic evaluator into ScreenRenderer for component
      visibility and button enabled state.

Context:
- SDUIComponent already has optional `visible: SDUIConditional` field
- ScreenRenderer currently ignores `visible` — should skip rendering when
  evaluateRule returns false
- ButtonAdapter should process `enabled: { rule: SDUIRule }` in props —
  disable button when rule evaluates to false
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Update existing ScreenRenderer.test.tsx and adapters.test.tsx
- Add Storybook demo showing conditional visibility toggle

Deliverable: Components with `visible` conditionals are hidden/shown based on
context. Buttons with `enabled` rules are disabled/enabled dynamically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.2 — Input Validation Rules
- [ ] Implement validation rule execution for SDUI input components.

Context:
- RFC section 3.5 defines validation pattern:
  { "validation": { "rules": [{ "rule": {...}, "message": "error text" }] } }
- Types exist: SDUIValidationRule, SDUIValidation
- PointsInputAdapter has textError prop but no validation integration
- Needs: validateField(value, validation: SDUIValidation, context): string | null
  (returns first failing rule's message, or null if valid)
- Depends on: SP_0006.0 (evaluateRule)
- Should integrate with PointsInputAdapter's error display
- Scope: validation utility function only. Form-level orchestration (when to
  trigger: blur/change/submit) is a separate concern for action handlers
- Add Storybook demo showing validation in action

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/validation.ts with validateField() +
integration into PointsInputAdapter + unit tests.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# SP_0006.3 — Rich Text Adapter
- [ ] Implement the rich_text component adapter described in RFC section 3.3.

Context:
- RFC defines rich text as array of typed nodes:
  - text: { type: "text", value: string, bold?: boolean, italic?: boolean, color?: string }
  - link: { type: "link", text: string, to: string, trackEvent?: string }
  - icon: { type: "icon", name: string, color?: string, size?: number }
  - linebreak: { type: "linebreak" }
- Types already defined: SDUIRichText, SDUITextNode, SDUILinkNode, SDUIIconNode,
  SDUILineBreakNode (in models/index.ts)
- Should render inline — all nodes in a single wrapping Text component
  (React Native supports nested Text for inline formatting)
- Links should use DS Text with underline style (similar to LinkAdapter)
- Icons use DS Icon component inline
- Linebreak nodes render as `\n` inside Text (not DOM br element —
  React Native Text supports newline characters)
- Register as "rich_text" type in adapter registry
- Add mock payload using rich_text to test inline formatting

Deliverable: RichTextAdapter registered in SDUI registry + unit tests +
Storybook demo showing mixed bold/link/icon inline text.
